info.name: Smart Home API
info.description:
Collection for testing the Smart Home ecosystem running via docker-compose.

Services:
- smart_home app (REST): http://localhost:8080
- temperature-api (REST): http://localhost:8081
- device-service (gRPC): localhost:8082
- telemetry-service (gRPC): localhost:8083

Quick start:
  cd apps && docker-compose up --build

Verification flow:
1. Run 'Health Check'
2. Run 'Create Sensor' (happy path)
3. Run 'Get All Sensors' — see live temperature
4. Run 'Update Sensor Value' with value=22.5
5. Run 'Update Sensor Value — Extreme (triggers anomaly)' with value=50
6. Run fail-path requests to verify error handling

Note: device-service and telemetry-service expose gRPC on ports 8082/8083.
Use grpcurl for testing them directly:
  grpcurl -plaintext localhost:8082 list
  grpcurl -plaintext localhost:8083 list

request.description: Check if the smart_home app is running

Create a new temperature sensor. Also fires-and-forgets a gRPC RegisterDevice call to device-service (if available). After creation, call 'Get All Sensors' to see live temperature from temperature-api.
Create a second sensor to verify multiple sensors work

exec: pm.test('Each sensor has required fields', function () {

request.description: Retrieve all sensors with live temperature data from temperature-api. Call multiple times — temperature value changes on each request.

exec: pm.test('Sensor has all required fields', function () {

request.description: Retrieve a specific sensor by ID with live temperature
Update an existing sensor's metadata
Update sensor value to a normal reading (22.5). Also publishes a telemetry measurement to Kafka (fire-and-forget).

exec: pm.test('Status code is 200 — zero value accepted', function () {

request.description: Edge case: value=0.0 must be accepted (not rejected as 'missing'). Uses *float64 pointer internally.
Extreme value (50 > threshold 40). Publishes to Kafka, which triggers anomaly detection in telemetry-service. The anomaly is stored in TimescaleDB and published to the anomaly.detected topic.
Get live temperature for a location via the smart_home app (proxied to temperature-api)
Delete the second sensor (Kitchen) to verify delete works

item.name: Get Sensor — Invalid ID (not a number)

request.description: Attempt to get a sensor with a non-numeric ID. Expects 400 Bad Request.

Create sensor without the required 'name' field. Expects 400 Bad Request.
Create sensor without the required 'type' field. Expects 400 Bad Request.
Create sensor with an empty JSON body. Expects 400 Bad Request.
Update sensor with non-numeric ID. Expects 400.

request.description: Update sensor value without the required 'value' field. Expects 400 Bad Request.

request.description: Update sensor value without the required 'status' field. Expects 400 Bad Request.

request.description: Update value of a sensor that doesn't exist. Expects 404 Not Found.
Delete a sensor that doesn't exist. Expects 404 Not Found.
Delete sensor with non-numeric ID. Expects 400 Bad Request.
Get a random temperature reading for a given location. Returns a new random value on each call (range: 15.0-35.0).

Get temperature for Bedroom location
Get temperature for Kitchen location
